summary.proof_note: Accio Work wins by joining market demand, supplier proof, landed-cost math, risk gates, ROI, and audit-ready output in one workflow.

dimensions.label: Decision quality
dimensions.why_it_matters: Ranks products with demand, risk, margin, and supplier signals instead of isolated facts.

dimensions.label: Landed-cost depth
dimensions.why_it_matters: Turns factory quotes into buy-plan economics with freight, tariffs, fees, inspection, and cash need.

dimensions.label: Supplier evidence
dimensions.why_it_matters: Preserves verification, compliance, defect, lead-time, capacity, and evidence references.

dimensions.label: Accio workflow fit
dimensions.why_it_matters: Fits the Accio sourcing path from discovery through supplier shortlist and purchase planning.

dimensions.label: Auditability
dimensions.why_it_matters: Explains assumptions and outputs a deterministic record a buyer can review or share.

dimensions.label: Time-to-buy-plan
dimensions.why_it_matters: Compresses research into a practical 90-day buy plan with ROI and risk gates.

alternatives.name: Accio Work buy-plan copilot
alternatives.verdict: 10x proof: integrated sourcing intelligence beats point tools by turning supplier evidence into a buy decision.

scores.rationale: Combines demand, landed cost, supplier risk, and ROI into one recommendation.
Models freight, tariffs, payment fees, inspection, marketplace fees, fulfillment, and cash need.
Keeps supplier verification, compliance, lead time, defect, and source evidence references with each candidate.
Native fit for Accio-style sourcing, supplier comparison, and buy-plan handoff.
Deterministic JSON makes assumptions, scoring, and proof points reviewable.
Moves from product shortlist to risk-gated 90-day plan in one CLI flow.

gaps: Needs live marketplace integrations beyond the deterministic demo fixture.
Human buyer still approves final supplier outreach and PO size.

alternatives.name: Spreadsheet sourcing workflow
alternatives.verdict: Useful as a control case, but not a 10x workflow for fast supplier-backed decisions.

scores.rationale: Flexible, but decisions depend on manual formulas and fragmented tabs.
Can model landed cost, but fields and assumptions are easy to omit.
Evidence usually lives in links, emails, and notes outside the sheet.
Requires copy-paste work to move from discovery into Accio-style sourcing actions.
Rows can be reviewed, but provenance and scoring logic drift over time.
Slow to convert research into an executable buy plan.

gaps: No built-in risk or ROI gate.

alternatives.name: Search-only product research
alternatives.verdict: Good top-of-funnel input, weak final-mile proof.

scores.rationale: Finds signals but leaves synthesis, tradeoffs, and final ranking to the buyer.
Usually lacks PO-level freight, tariff, inspection, and fee math.
Can surface suppliers, but does not normalize operational evidence.
Discovery output must be rebuilt before it is actionable in Accio workflows.
Search results change and often lack a durable decision record.
Fast for finding ideas, slow for converting them into a purchase plan.

alternatives.name: Generic AI agent
alternatives.verdict: Powerful assistant layer, but not a commerce-specific moat by itself.

scores.rationale: Can summarize and draft, but commerce scoring depends on prompt quality and tool setup.
May calculate costs when prompted, but does not enforce complete landed-cost coverage.
Often mixes cited and inferred supplier claims unless evidence rules are built in.
Needs custom glue to mirror Accio sourcing stages and buyer handoffs.
Reasoning may be hard to reproduce without structured exports and fixed assumptions.
Can accelerate research, but final plan quality varies by orchestration.

alternatives.name: Ecommerce product research tool
alternatives.verdict: Closest point-tool competitor, but the buy-plan gap remains open.

scores.rationale: Strong market and listing views, but supplier execution is often separate.
May estimate margins, yet detailed import and inspection assumptions are limited.
Can include supplier or listing context, but not always operational verification depth.
Built for product analytics more than Accio supplier workflow handoff.
Dashboards are useful, but scoring inputs are not always exportable or explainable.
Speeds product validation while leaving supplier-risk and PO planning work.

gaps: Market insight is stronger than supplier execution proof.

recommended_proof: Show the buyer a ranked alternative map, not just a product list.
Attach supplier evidence and risk gates to every buy-plan recommendation.
Expose landed-cost assumptions deeply enough for PO sizing and margin review.
Export deterministic JSON so pitch judges can inspect the proof path.
